themes.hook: India is currently the only major economy that can sail through the Strait of Hormuz while the rest of the world pays a 'war tax' on every barrel.
key_data_points: Selective blockade exclusion could allow India to restore nearly 7 million barrels of oil supply per day.
Delhi has already capped commercial LPG supply at 20% of average consumption to manage volatility.
Energy is the single largest cost component for India's ceramic (Morbi) and wood panel industries, directly impacting export margins.
themes.non_obvious_angle: While the world focuses on 'energy prices,' the real story is India's 'diplomatic discount.' By securing safe passage for vessels like the Shivalik and Nanda Devi, India is weaponizing its neutrality to maintain a manufacturing edge that China and the US currently can't match.
themes.your_take_prompt: Kashish, do you believe India's 'strategic autonomy' in the Middle East is a sustainable long-term economic moat, or are we just one maritime accident away from a fiscal deficit crisis?

source_articles.title: GLOBAL ENERGY FLASH: INDIA MOVES TO STABILIZE HORMUZ CRISIS
Delhi government caps commercial LPG supply at 20%
Iran allowing India to pass through Strait of Hormuz

themes.hook: The era of the 'loss-making startup' IPO is officially over. SEBI just cleared the runway for the real heavyweights to take over the exchanges.
key_data_points: New SEBI rules allow large IPOs with lesser stake dilution and longer compliance windows.
IDBI Bank privatization stalled as financial bids fell below the government's reserve price.
The Jio IPO is being positioned as a watershed moment for Indian capital market liquidity.
themes.non_obvious_angle: SEBI isn't just 'updating' rules; they are effectively creating a 'VIP Lane' for massive conglomerates (Reliance, Tata) to list their retail and digital arms. This signals a shift from 'growth-at-all-costs' venture-backed listings to 'systemically important' cash-flow listings.
themes.your_take_prompt: What's your take on the 'valuation gap' in PSU sales? Is the government being too optimistic, or is the market failing to price in the 'legacy' value of assets like IDBI?

source_articles.title: SEBI CHAIRMAN Says Govt has notified the rules for large IPOs
Jio IPO Dilution Rule Change – Timeline
IDBI Bank disinvestment unlikely to proceed

themes.hook: HDFC Bank is trading at its cheapest valuation in 20 years (1.7x FY28e P/B), yet the smart money is rotating into 'boring' PSU banks. The private bank premium is dead.
key_data_points: Bajaj Finance growth is moderating, leading to a significant valuation derating.
NBFCs face a massive credit gap for MSMEs due to rising short-term debt costs.
PSU banks are outperforming private peers in credit growth for the first time in a decade.
themes.non_obvious_angle: Private banks are becoming 'utilities'—safe, slow, and regulated—while PSU banks are the ones actually funding the high-risk, high-reward industrial capex and infrastructure cycle. The 'quality' trade has shifted from the balance sheet to the order book.
themes.your_take_prompt: Kashish, as someone at Zerodha watching retail flows, are you seeing investors finally abandon the 'HDFC/Bajaj' safety net for PSU volatility?

source_articles.title: BAJAJ FINANCE: GROWTH MODERATES, VAL DERATES
Case for P/B compression for private banks?
India’s Credit Paradox – NBFCs & MSME Credit Gap

themes.hook: We spent a decade obsessed with the 'India Stack' for payments. Now, the 'India Energy Stack' is about to do the same for our power grid.
key_data_points: Waaree Energies is building a 10 GW integrated ingot and wafer facility, the largest in India.
IEX is seeing a structural shift from Day-Ahead Markets (DAM) to Real-Time Markets (RTM).
Transmission capacity, not generation, is the new bottleneck as peak demand surges.
themes.non_obvious_angle: The 'Green Transition' isn't a solar panel story; it's a software and transmission story. The winners won't be the companies making the energy, but the ones managing the 'Real-Time' volatility of the grid as we move away from coal.
themes.your_take_prompt: Does the 'Energy Stack' concept give India a better shot at Net Zero than the US/Europe models, given our centralized digital infrastructure?

source_articles.title: Electrifying effect of India Energy Stack
IEX Volume mix shift across FY23–FY25

themes.hook: Indian IT services aren't dying because of AI. They are being cannibalized from the inside by their own global clients.
key_data_points: High-end tech talent is abandoning IT services firms for Global Capability Centres (GCCs).
Career mobility is driving record tenant churn in Bengaluru and Gurgaon rental markets.
IIT/IISc incubators are pivoting funding toward 'Deep-Tech' over consumer-facing apps.
themes.non_obvious_angle: This isn't just a job shift; it’s a structural 'hollowing out' of the Indian IT middle class. GCCs pay more but hire fewer, creating a 'K-shaped' recovery in urban real estate where luxury demand stays high while mid-tier rentals collapse.
themes.your_take_prompt: Is the era of the 'mass-recruiter' IT giant over? How does this change the 'India Growth' thesis if our biggest job engine stalls?

source_articles.title: India’s tech talent increasingly turns to GCCs
Career mobility drives tenant churn in rental markets
IISc incubator to up funding for deep-tech
